MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared for the Plaintiffs Myrtle Susie Sweider, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Richard Sweider, Sr., deceased; and Richard Sweider, Jr.; Kelli Griffith, Trena Vick; as wrongful death heirs of Richard Sweider, Sr., deceased ("plaintiffs").

Witness Myrtle Susie Sweider and Kelli Griffith appeared by Court Call, sworn and testified.
No appearance by Plaintiffs Richard Sweider, Jr., Steven Vick, Darrell Nagel, Dannyell Colisch, Matthew Moore, or Justin Moore.
After hearing the testimony and considered the evidence both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, the following are entitled to distribution of net funds obtained by Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds (both litigation funds and funds from claims against bankrupt asbestos defendant trusts) in the following proportions:

Estate of Richard Sweider, Sr. 0%
Myrtle Susie Sweider 40%
Richard Sweider, Jr. 15%
Kelli Griffith 15%
Estate of Lisa Biesanz 15%
Estate of Trena Vick 15%
The Signature of plaintiff Myrtle Susie Sweider alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.
Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Edward P. Tugade of Demler Armstrong & Rowland LLP (415.757.3493), appearing for Defendant Pneumo Amex / moving party.
Patrick Campbell of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555), appearing for Plaintiff / opposing party Myrtle Susie Sweider, as Successor-In-Interest and as Wrongful Death Heir of Richard Sweider, Sr., Deceased.

RULING:
DEFENDANT PNEUMO ABEX LLC, SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO ABEX CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Exhibits A, B and G and denied as to Exhibits C, D, E and F. Pursuant to C.C.P. 438, defendant failed to demonstrate that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against it because defendant failed to demonstrate plaintiffs' causes of action against it are time-barred by C.C.P. 340.2. Plaintiffs' complaint for wrongful death and survival was timely filed within the statute of limitations period. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment substituting defendant for a Doe does not relate back to the filing of that complaint because it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant's true name or the facts rendering it liable when the complaint was filed. This conclusion is the same even if the Court considers the substance of decedent's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' standard interrogatory responses. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Court Reporter: Tara L. Williams, CSR #13218. (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Brayton Purcell LLP's motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Trena Vick is denied as untimely. Brayton failed to provide plaintiff with proper notice as required by C.C.P. section 1005(b), which requires all moving and supporting papers to be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing, which notice period is increased by 10 calendar days if the notice is served by mail to a place outside California. Here, Brayton provided plaintiff notice by mail to an address in Oregon on August 7, 2015, which did not provide proper notice. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
